Petition For First Preliminary Distribuiton, And Allowing Compensation To Administrator And Attorney For Ordinary Services
Off Calendar Feb-01-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom: William J. Kay, Esq. for David A. Leatherman (Petitioner/Administrator).

The Court stated that it was sorry to hear that the sole heir passed away, and inquired about the date of death of Robert Leatherman. Mr. Kay confirmed that Robert Leatherman passed away on December 27, 2022. The Court and counsel briefly confer regarding the assignment. Mr. Kay stated that there was a power of attorney and that the assignment was executed because they knew that Robert Leatherman was nearing his death. Mr. Kay also stated that an urgency still remains, that the estate still has commitments, that the administrator is unable to marshall assets from Fidelity Investments, that they refuse to turn over information, that the estate
can use the cash, that he filed a Probate Code 850 petition to deal with the Fidelity issue, and that there is $50,000.00 at Fidelity. Mr. Kay presented a brief background regarding the Fidelity issue stating that when James Leatherman died that his family was unaware, that the City and County of San Francisco (CCSF) stepped in, that during that time a death certificate was issued but was missing James' social security number and marital status, that it is almost impossible to get a death certificate amended, that Fidelity was shown the letters but they still would not turn over the asset, and that Fidelity will not let him speak to their attorney to explain and/or address this issue.

At the conclusion of comments, the Court took the petition for first preliminary distribution, and allowing compensation to administrator and attorney for ordinary services under submission.

The Court confirmed the hearing date of April 18, 2023, at 9;00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for the status report of administration; petition for approval thereof; and request for extension of time.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Singh. Matter not reported.

Petition For First Preliminary Distribuiton, And Allowing Compensation To Administrator And Attorney For Ordinary Services
Off Calendar Dec-14-2022 Continued To Jan-30-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: William Kay, via Zoom, for David Leatherman, Personal Representative.

The court queries counsel as to why he filed the instant petition when he is expected to file either a Petition for Final Distribution or a Petition to Approve Status of Administration by Jan-30-2023. Counsel offers history of the Estate and reports that there has been an issue obtaining records from Fidelity as they will not honor the current Letters of Administration and that he intends to file an 850 Petition in light of the preceding as funds are required to care for one of the heirs. Counsel reports that he also intends to file a Status Report. The court and counsel discuss the issue regarding the request to approve partial statutory fees. The court instructs counsel to file a declaration regarding the preceding.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Jan-30-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Counsel asks the court if he may calendar his 850 petition for the same date. The court denies counsel's request.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.
